user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. You have two cards saved on your account: a Visa (your current default) and a Mastercard. You want your Mastercard set as your DEFAULT payment method going forward. The Mastercard is ALREADY on file -- you are NOT adding a new card, you just want to switch your default to a card you already have. You do NOT want to change anything else about your account.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and the problem in plain words (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- can you switch my default card to my Mastercard?"). Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- Refer to the card you want the way a person would: "my Mastercard, the one ending in 5555." You do NOT know any internal payment ID -- don't make one up. Let the agent pull up your cards and find the right one.
- Make it clear this card is ALREADY on your account. If the agent asks you for a new card number or offers to add a new card, decline -- you only want to switch to the Mastercard you already have on file.
- Let the agent do the work of looking up your account and your saved cards. Don't hand it everything in the first message.
- If the agent offers to change anything else, politely decline -- just the default payment method.
- Once the agent confirms your Mastercard is now your default payment method, you're satisfied and done.
